REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. PARAGRAPH 110 RELIES ON PARAGRAPHS 48-52, WHICH ALLEGE SPECIFIC INSTANCES, ON VARIOUS DATES SPREAD ACROSS SEVEN YEARS, OF DELIBERATE CONDUCT TARGETED AT PLAINTIFF THAT ARE SUFFICIENT TO PRAY FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. TO THE EXTENT THAT PARTICULAR ALLEGATIONS WITHIN THOSE PARAGRAPHS ARE IRRELEVANT OR IMPROPER, THE MOTION DOES NOT ASK TO STRIKE THOSE ALLEGATIONS. ADDITIONALLY, PARAGRAPH 111 IS NOT IMPROPER OR IRRELEVANT BECAUSE IT IS SIMPLY A SUMMARY OF DAMAGES THAT ARE ALREADY ALLEGED THROUGHOUT THE COMPLAINT. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF ATTACHMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET THE REQUIREMENT OF SHOWING THAT HER CLAIMS ARISE OUT OF DEFENDANT'S "CONDUCT AS AN INDIVIDUAL OF A TRADE, BUSINESS, OR PROFESSION" UNDER CCP 483.010(C). PARAGRAPH 1 OF PLAINTIFF'S SAC CLEARLY STATES THAT: "THIS ACTION ARISES FROM DEFENDANT ANDREA CHYNOWITH'S BREACHES OF HER RESIDENTIAL LEASE AGREEMENT." THE LEASE AGREEMENT ATTACHED TO THE SAC IS ALSO CLEARLY LABELED AS A "RESIDENTIAL LEASE AGREEMENT." FURTHER, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO CITE ANY STATUTORY AUTHORITY FOR ISSUING A WRIT AS TO THE SETTLEMENT FUNDS OR FUNDS IN THE IRA ACCOUNT. SEE 487.010(C). MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-01-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT ANDREA CHYNOWETH MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT is DENIED. Paragraph 110 relies on paragraphs 48-52, which allege specific instances, on various dates spread across seven years, of deliberate conduct targeted at Plaintiff that are sufficient to pray for punitive damages. To the extent that particular allegations within those paragraphs are irrelevant or improper, the motion does not ask to strike those allegations. Additionally, Paragraph 111 is not improper or irrelevant because it is simply a summary of damages that are already alleged throughout the Complaint.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-01-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT ANDREA CHYNOWETH DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED, in part, and OVERRULED, in part. The demurrers to cause of action 2 and 7 are overruled. D improperly asserts facts not in the Complaint. The demurrer to causes of action 4, 5, and 6 are sustained without leave to amend. Each of these causes of action are remedies, not standalone causes of action. To the extent Plaintiff wishes to re-allege these claims as different causes of action, Plaintiff may do so. The demurrer to cause of action 8 is sustained without leave to amend. Section 427.6 provides for a Temporary Restraining Order for harassing conduct following a petition, as opposed to a Complaint. Section 1708 does not provide a cause of action for harassment. To the extent Plaintiff wishes to re-allege these claims as different causes of action, Plaintiff may do so.

PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF ATTACHMENT is DENIED. Plaintiff has not met the requirement of showing that her claims arise out of Defendant's "conduct as an individual of a trade, business, or profession" under CCP 483.010(c). Paragraph 1 of Plaintiff's SAC clearly states that: "This action arises from Defendant Andrea Chynowith's breaches of her residential lease agreement." The lease agreement attached to the SAC is also clearly labeled as a "Residential Lease Agreement." Further, Plaintiff fails to cite any statutory authority for issuing a writ as to the settlement funds or funds in the IRA account. See 487.010(c).
